title: TOBACCO PREVENTION AND EDUCATION PROGRAM - 211info
text: GRANT COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT TOBACCO PREVENTION AND EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Oregon Tobacco Prevention and Education Program works with diverse partners statewide to promote smoke-free environments and communities, reduce the influence of tobacco product marketing, and encourage tobacco users to quit. These programs engage their communities and challenge them to become healthier through preventing tobacco use. 528 E Main Street Suite E John Day, OR 97845 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 575-0429 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 575-0429 Russ Comer Hotline (800) 784-8669 Quit Line Hotline (877) 266-3863 Spanish Quit Line TTY/TTD (877) 777-6534 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Grant County Services: Comprehensive Information and Referral * Smoking/Nicotine Addiction Issues Smoking/Vaping Cessation * Smoking/Nicotine Addiction Issues Smoking/Vaping Cessation Support * Smoking/Nicotine Addiction Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER REGULATION - VITAL RECORDS GENERAL MEDICAL CARE - GRANT COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT RURAL HEALTH CLINIC HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - SCREENWISE-ENROLLING HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - STD CLINIC HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON MOTHERSCARE HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - PUBLIC HEALTH MODERNIZATION HUMAN REPRODUCTION - OREGON REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH PROGRAM CLINIC INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - BABIES FIRST! Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
